title: Database connection and table creation
user_visible_behavior: Running init.sh creates the cities and search_history tables in PostgreSQL without errors
verification: Run: python -m py_compile backend/db.py
Run: python -c "from backend.db import init_db; init_db(); print('OK')"
Connect to PostgreSQL and confirm both tables exist
evidence: python -m py_compile backend/db.py → Syntax OK. Full DB verification runs on droplet via deploy.sh.
notes: Use psycopg2 with RealDictCursor. Read DATABASE_URL from environment. No ORM.

title: Open-Meteo geocoding and weather fetch
user_visible_behavior: Given a city name, the app resolves lat/lon and returns temperature, feels-like, humidity, wind speed, and a weather description
verification: Run: python -m py_compile backend/weather.py
Run: python -c "import asyncio; from backend.weather import geocode, fetch_weather; print(asyncio.run(geocode('Tokyo')))"
Confirm lat, lon, and country code are returned
evidence: geocode('Tokyo') → {name: Tokyo, country: JP, lat: 35.6895, lon: 139.69171}. fetch_weather() → {temperature_c: 21.2, feels_like_c: 25.2, humidity_pct: 99, wind_speed_kmh: 2.2, weather_desc: Mainly clear}
notes: Use httpx async client. Geocoding endpoint: geocoding-api.open-meteo.com. Weather endpoint: api.open-meteo.com. Translate WMO weather codes to human labels.

title: POST /api/search endpoint
user_visible_behavior: Posting a city name returns weather data as JSON and saves it to the database
verification: Run: python -m py_compile backend/main.py
Start server: uvicorn backend.main:app --port 8000
Run: curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/search -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"city": "London"}' | python -m json.tool
Confirm temperature_c, humidity_pct, weather_desc are in the response
Confirm the row exists in search_history table
evidence: python -m py_compile backend/main.py → Syntax OK. Full curl verification runs once DB is live on droplet.
notes: Upsert cities using ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE. Return 400 for empty city, 404 for unknown city, 502 for API failure, 500 for DB error.

title: GET /api/history endpoint
user_visible_behavior: The history endpoint returns a list of past searches including city name and country from the cities table
verification: Run: curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/history | python -m json.tool
Confirm each item includes city, country, temperature_c, and searched_at
Confirm the SQL query uses a JOIN between search_history and cities
evidence: python -m py_compile backend/main.py → Syntax OK. JOIN query: search_history JOIN cities ON city_id. curl verification pending DB on droplet.
notes: Return last 50 results ordered by searched_at DESC. Must use a JOIN — this is a grading requirement.

title: Vanilla JS frontend
user_visible_behavior: Opening localhost:8000 shows a search bar. Searching a city displays the weather result. The history table below updates automatically.
verification: Open http://localhost:8000 in a browser
Search for 'Paris' and confirm weather data appears
Confirm the history table shows the new entry
Search for a fake city and confirm an error message appears
evidence: frontend/index.html created — single file, no frameworks. Search posts to /api/search, renders result card, refreshes history table. Error states handled. Browser verification pending on droplet.
notes: Single file at frontend/index.html. No frameworks, no npm. Fetch from /api/search and /api/history.

title: DigitalOcean deployment with systemd
user_visible_behavior: The app is live at http://137.184.108.210:8000 and survives a server reboot
verification: weather-recorder.service file exists in project root
scripts/deploy.sh exists and contains all setup steps
Run locally: curl -s http://137.184.108.210:8000/api/history
Confirm HTTP 200 response from the live server
SSH in and run: systemctl status weather-recorder — confirm active (running)
SSH in and run: sudo reboot, wait 60s, then curl again to confirm it survived reboot
notes: See docs/deploy.md for server details and full step-by-step instructions. Credentials are not stored in source control.

title: README and AI workflow documentation
user_visible_behavior: README explains how to set up and run the project locally and on the server. AI_WORKFLOW.md documents how Claude Code was used.
verification: README.md exists with: setup steps, environment variables, how to run locally, how to deploy
AI_WORKFLOW.md exists with: tools used, prompts used per feature, what was reviewed manually
A Future Improvements section exists in README
evidence: README.md and AI_WORKFLOW.md created. README covers setup, endpoints, schema, deployment, API choice, trade-offs, and future improvements. AI_WORKFLOW.md documents specific prompts and workflows used with Claude Code.
notes: Graders explicitly want to know how AI tools were used. AI_WORKFLOW.md is a required deliverable.
